key_insight: A hybrid-DFT database of >2,500 stable halide double perovskites reveals rich spin-polarized electronic structures, including visible-gap and half-metallic candidates.
engineering_relevance: The dataset accelerates screening of perovskites for photovoltaics, photocatalysis, radiation detection, and spintronic/magnetic applications.
method: Computed spin-polarized density of states for all Cs2BB'X6 compounds predicted stable by the τ tolerance factor.
Used hybrid-functional DFT to generate electronic-structure data across X = I, Br, Cl, and F.
Applied LOBSTER bonding analysis plus UMAP visualization and an interactive app for exploration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports >2,500 compounds, with 719 visible-band-gap materials and 118 half-metals, supported by hybrid-DFT and bonding-analysis data.
watch_item: Stability and property trends are screened computationally, so experimental synthesis feasibility and finite-temperature effects may still limit real-world performance.
paper_title: Spin-Polarized Electronic Structure and Chemical Bonding Data for 2,500+ Halide Double Perovskites
